description: MQTT_Kafka_Bridge — Aggregates ISA-95 MQTT messages (OT, single-attribute) into Kafka records (IT, full-object). Bridges OT→IT boundary.

aggregation.description: Collect all BDE attributes for a machine within 5s window, flush as single Kafka record
Tool events are infrequent — publish on change, deduplicate within 10s
Maintenance status changes rarely — publish on change
ERP order changes are event-driven — publish on change

window.description: Collect all MQTT messages for a groupBy key within windowMs, then flush as single Kafka record with all collected attributes merged into one JSON object.
window.behavior: Timer starts on first message for a key. All subsequent messages within the window update the same record. On flush: publish to Kafka, reset buffer.
window.missingAttributes: If an attribute was not received within the window, it is omitted from the Kafka record (sparse object).

on_change.description: Publish to Kafka immediately when any attribute changes. Deduplicate identical messages within deduplicateMs.
on_change.behavior: Compare incoming value with last published value. If different (or first message): aggregate all known attributes for this key and publish. If same: skip (dedup).
on_change.missingAttributes: Uses last-known values for attributes not in the current message (stateful).

recordFormat.description: Each Kafka record produced by the bridge
recordFormat.key: Entity ID extracted from MQTT topic (e.g. 'SGM-004', 'KA202657129')

value._mqtt_messages: Number of MQTT messages aggregated into this record
value._topic_prefix: Common MQTT topic prefix for traceability
value....attributes: All SM attributes collected within the aggregation window

notes.purpose: Bridges the OT/IT boundary: OT systems publish fast, small MQTT messages. IT systems consume structured, complete Kafka records.
notes.data_flow: OPC-UA/PLC → MQTT Broker (ISA-95 topics) → Bridge → Kafka → KG Builder / Data Lake / BI
notes.scalability: Bridge can run as multiple instances with MQTT shared subscriptions ($share/bridge-group/) for horizontal scaling.
notes.ordering: Kafka ordering is guaranteed per partition key (= entity ID). Bridge preserves MQTT message order within each aggregation window.
notes.back_pressure: If Kafka is slow, bridge buffers in memory. configurable max buffer size before dropping oldest messages.
